projects.name: 1991 Mazda Miata

sections.title: Overview
sections.text: With the goal of learning the basics of 3D modeling, projection designing and assembly mates, I undertook the task of modeling my favorite car, the 1991 MX-5 in SolidWorks. Using reference pictures, I modeled each static panel of the chassis. I then created separate components for moving parts such as the swing doors, spinning wheels, the hydraulic trunk and the iconic winking headlights. While not an engineering-precise drawing, the model is complete enough to be exported and rendered, opening the door to creative applications such as advertisement visuals, producing 3D printed miniatures and more.

sections.title: Process
sections.text:
I began by projecting to-scale reference images of the front, side and top views onto their corresponding axes in SolidWorks. Using the 3D sketch feature, I was then able to draw freely in space rather than being confined to a single plane. By locking the camera to one reference view at a time, I sketched each body panel along that projection, then switched to another view to adjust and vary the curvature. This created a 3D boundary that curved and bent in space according to all three references simultaneously.

	To fill these boundaries into actual surfaces, I used Surface Loft and Fill Surface, then applied Thicken to give each panel its physical form. This process was repeated for each non-moving body panel on one half of the car, which was then mirrored to complete the symmetry. Separate components such as the wheels, steering wheel, trunk, seats and headlights, were modeled independently using extrusions built up from basic shapes and brought together in the final assembly.

projects.shortDescription: A 3D assembly of a 1991 Mazda Miata modeled in SolidWorks

projects.name: Project Beta

sections.title: Overview
sections.text: A detailed 3D model of a medieval castle with hand-sculpted stonework and intricate battlements.

sections.title: Process
sections.text: High-poly sculpt baked down to game-ready topology, textured with PBR materials in Substance Painter.

sections.title: Outcome
sections.text: Delivered as a real-time asset with LODs suitable for Unreal Engine environments.

projects.shortDescription: A detailed 3D model of a medieval castle with hand-sculpted stonework and intricate battlements.

projects.name: Project Gamma

sections.title: Overview
sections.text: A 3D model of a modern architectural building exploring minimalist concrete and glass design.

sections.title: Process
sections.text: Modelled to architectural specification with accurate material properties and natural lighting simulation.

sections.title: Outcome
sections.text: Photorealistic visualisation used for client presentation and planning approval.

projects.shortDescription: A 3D model of a modern architectural building exploring minimalist concrete and glass design.

projects.name: Project Delta

sections.title: Overview
sections.text: A 3D model of a space station orbiting Earth, designed with modular docking bays and solar arrays.

sections.title: Process
sections.text: Hard-surface modelling with kitbash components, textured to simulate weathered metal and worn panels.

sections.title: Outcome
sections.text: Rendered in a deep-space HDRI environment with motion blur for cinematic stills.

projects.shortDescription: A 3D model of a space station orbiting Earth, designed with modular docking bays and solar arrays.

projects.name: Project Epsilon

sections.title: Overview
sections.text: A 3D model of an ancient temple with moss-covered columns and crumbling stonework.

sections.title: Process
sections.text: Environment built with scatter foliage, decal-based wear, and physically-based erosion maps.

sections.title: Outcome
sections.text: Atmospheric scene lit with golden-hour HDRI and volumetric god-rays for dramatic effect.

projects.shortDescription: A 3D model of an ancient temple with moss-covered columns and crumbling stonework.

projects.name: Project Zeta

sections.title: Overview
sections.text: A 3D model of a futuristic vehicle blending aerodynamic form with heavy-duty utility design.

sections.title: Process
sections.text: Designed from concept sketches into a clean NURBS surface model then converted to subdivision mesh.

sections.title: Outcome
sections.text: Studio-lit product render with a turntable animation for portfolio presentation.

projects.shortDescription: A 3D model of a futuristic vehicle blending aerodynamic form with heavy-duty utility design.

projects.name: Project Eta

sections.title: Overview
sections.text: A 3D model of a fantasy creature with organic musculature and intricate scale detail.

sections.title: Process
sections.text: ZBrush sculpt with dynamesh for primary forms, then refined with layers for secondary and tertiary detail.

sections.title: Outcome
sections.text: Rigged and posed for a hero shot render, with subsurface scattering skin shader for realism.

projects.shortDescription: A 3D model of a fantasy creature with organic musculature and intricate scale detail.

projects.name: Project Theta

sections.title: Overview
sections.text: A 3D model of a modern skyscraper with parametric facade patterning and reflective curtain-wall glazing.

sections.title: Process
sections.text: Procedural facade generated via Houdini, exported as geometry cache into the Blender scene.

sections.title: Outcome
sections.text: Dusk render with interior light bloom and city-reflection in the glass panels.

projects.shortDescription: A 3D model of a modern skyscraper with parametric facade patterning and reflective curtain-wall glazing.

projects.name: Project Iota

sections.title: Overview
sections.text: A 3D model of an underwater scene with bioluminescent coral, kelp forests and caustic lighting.

sections.title: Process
sections.text: Volume scatter shader used for water medium, caustics projected via light texture maps.

sections.title: Outcome
sections.text: Final animation loop rendered in Cycles at 60 fps for use as an ambient background installation.

projects.shortDescription: A 3D model of an underwater scene with bioluminescent coral, kelp forests and caustic lighting.

sections.title: Who I Am
sections.text:
Hi! I'm currently studying Mechanical Engineering at Concordia expected to graduate early 2029. I have a background in web development and information technology. Originally from Vancouver, BC, I moved to Montreal in search of opportunity and to make connections.

	My interest for technology started at a young age, growing up alongside the rise of the internet. Building my first PC was the moment that truly kickstarted my journey. It inspired me to run a small refurbishing side hustle, sourcing cheap parts from the second-hand market, assembling them into complete machines and reselling them for a profit. That hands-on curiosity carried me into software, where I completed a coding bootcamp at BrainStation and took on freelance web development projects.

	I then earned my CompTIA+ certification and landed a role as an Implementation Technician at F3 Technologies, managing POS, networking and security for small-medium size restaurants across Vancouver. During this time, I also worked at Capilano University as a Maintenance Worker. To which I enrolled in their Engineering Transition Program, a decision that brought me back to my deeper passion for designing and problem solving in the physical world.

	That program reignited something in me. The design projects, CAD modeling, prototyping and the whole process of engineering a solution from research to manufacturing, I found myself genuinely excited in a way I hadn't felt academically before. That's what brought me to Montreal where I can make use of my French as well. Whether it's drafting a design or getting into the technical weeds, I'm at my best when I'm doing both. I'm always eager to learn new skills, hone current ones and gain experience in industry.

sections.title: What I Bring
sections.text:
A growing foundation in mechanical engineering with hands-on experience in CAD modeling, prototyping and the full design process from concept to solution. I approach problems methodically and enjoy the challenge of turning ideas into something tangible.

	A background in IT and web development gives me a versatile technical toolkit that most engineering students don't have. From building and networking computers to writing clean code, I'm comfortable working across both hardware and software environments.

	Having worked across multiple industries, from coordinating campus events with various departments to training clients on technical systems, I'm used to translating technical concepts for non-technical people and working effectively in team settings.

	I'm fluent in English, French and Vietnamese, giving me the ability to work comfortably in both of Canada's official languages and connect with a broader range of people and environments.

	The best way to get a sense of what I can do is to see it for yourself — take a look around the site and explore the projects I've worked on along the way.

Engineering: SolidWorks
Programming: C, C++, JavaScript, HTML, CSS
Development: Frontend & Backend, Git, MySQL
Productivity: Microsoft 365, Word, Excel, PowerPoint
Collaboration: Microsoft Teams

sections.title: What I'm Looking For
sections.text: I'm at a point in my journey where I want to get out of the classroom and into the real world. I'm eager to work alongside experienced professionals, learn how industry operates from the inside, and build meaningful connections along the way. Outside of school you'll find me training for triathlons, playing badminton or gathered around a board game with friends. If you think there's a fit, lets connect!
